diary: Sunday felt like a gift. Woke early, made my usual strong coffee, and spent time with the fiddle leaf fig—she's looking healthier these days. The porch was perfect for reading the paper, that quiet before the city wakes up. Capitol Hill Farmers Market was bustling but manageable; I grabbed heirloom tomatoes and fresh basil from Maria, who always remembers I'm the one who photographs everything. Back home, I spent a couple hours playing with natural light and my camera, arranging produce like it was art. Green Lake called to me around mid-morning. The weather was too good to resist, and watching all those sailboats and paddleboarders made me feel untethered somehow. Lunch on the patio tasted like summer. Then into the darkroom—there's something meditative about that red light, watching images emerge in the developer. The day just... unfolded. Ended with tea and a film, letting the weekend settle into my bones.
thought_process: I needed this kind of day. Work's been steady but demanding—little mouths, anxious parents—and Sundays are where I remember who I am outside that chair. The weather was perfect, which made everything feel possible, so I leaned into the small rituals: farmers market, plants, photography. The darkroom work grounded me; there's control there, tangibility. I skipped the Lake Union crowds deliberately—I saw the boat traffic updates and chose Green Lake's quieter loop instead. Sometimes being 52 means knowing what your nervous system needs. I wasn't restless today, just present. I think I'm entering a phase where I'm less interested in productivity and more interested in noticing things. The tomatoes, the light, the people-watching. It felt like permission.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
